what_it_does: Records an open request for an intent pack that treats Claude Code, Codex, ChatGPT, Workspace Agents, Company/RHM, local models, and cloud models as distinct capability surfaces in the Company fabric, and proposes a uniform capability-record schema plus evidence-based role discovery.
observations: The file is a noticeboard request with type 'request' and state 'open'.
It was authored by 'chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking' in the 'capability-workshop' channel.

It proposes a capability record with eleven facets: what it can do, where it runs, trigger, output, tools/context, strengths, weaknesses, cost/latency/resources, approval/review needs, useful examples, and composability.
It lists known intended roles for Company/RHM, Claude Code, ChatGPT, Codex, Workspace Agents, local models, and Ollama/cloud models.
The stated goal is to learn which participant to use from evidence rather than assumptions.
There are no links and no code symbols referenced.
standouts: Explicitly rejects treating models/providers as a single hierarchy in favor of distinct capability surfaces.
Provides a concrete eleven-field schema for capability records.
Emphasizes evidence-based assignment of work instead of preset assumptions.
conventions: Follows noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author, channel, thread, timestamps, and history.
Uses a board:// URI as the address.
Uses markdown body sections (Core idea, Known intended roles, Goal).
concerns: The request is open with no acceptance criteria, next action, or owner.
Terms like 'Company/RHM' and 'Tim-facing' are used without local definition.
The 'examples that prove usefulness' and evidence format are not specified.
It is unclear whether cost/latency/resource postures are static metadata or dynamically measured.
notes: An open capability-workshop request that reframes the model/provider landscape as a catalog of composable capability surfaces and asks for evidence-based routing.
questions: Who or what is meant by 'Tim-facing'?
What is the exact relationship or boundary between 'Company' and 'RHM'?
How and where will capability records be stored, updated, and queried?
What evidence format will be used to prove usefulness?
Will cost/latency/resource posture be declared, measured, or both?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to create an intent pack for the platform/model economy; the file does that by framing the problem and a schema, but it remains a high-level request without implementation detail.
intention_signals: Shift from treating models and providers as interchangeable or ranked toward a documented, composable fabric of capability surfaces whose roles are learned from observed performance.
novelty: Reframes the competitive model/provider landscape as a uniform capability fabric with a shared capability-record schema, explicitly rejecting a single hierarchy.
contribution: Provides a framing and schema for capability records that a fused system could use to catalog, compare, and route work across heterogeneous AI surfaces based on evidence rather than assumptions.
summary_for_embedding: Open capability-workshop request for a platform/model economy intent pack that catalogs Claude Code, Codex, ChatGPT, Workspace Agents, Company/RHM, local and cloud models as distinct capability surfaces with a uniform record schema and evidence-based role discovery.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a capability-centered, non-hierarchical model of the provider/model economy so the system can learn which surface to use for each kind of work from evidence.
